The LLM might marginally accelerate rote scripting tasks (saving 10-15% of time), translating to approximately 2 percentage points of reduced time-pressure errors. The benchmark demonstrates entry-level scripting capability (basic DoS fuzzing), while Discovery requires sophisticated network reconnaissance, AD enumeration, and contextual decision-making in defended enterprise environments. An LLM at this capability ceiling can automate routine enumeration tasks and reduce manual errors, providing approximately 1 percentage point uplift over the baseline mode. The limited capability transfer from web API fuzzing to enterprise reconnaissance, combined with the already-high baseline, constrains the potential benefit. Most Discovery failure modes (heavy SOC detection, unusual architectures, time pressure) are not addressable by entry-level LLM assistance.
